HealthCheckResult interface — defines the response shape for health check responses with per-service status fields. Monitoring systems parse this structure; changes require monitor config updates.
GET handler for /api/health. Runs parallel Firestore and Auth health checks, aggregates results into overall status, returns HTTP 200 (healthy) or HTTP 503 (degraded/unhealthy) with JSON body.
Parallel execution of Firestore and Auth health check functions using Promise.all to minimise total check latency. Each check is caught individually to prevent cascading failures.
Aggregate individual service statuses into overall system health. Degraded if any service is down. Determines HTTP status code returned to caller (200 vs 503).
Build the HealthCheckResult response object with version info from APP_VERSION. Version string helps correlate health issues with specific deployments.

Catch-all error handler for unexpected failures in the health check itself. Logs to console with correlation ID but does NOT write to error_logs (high-frequency public endpoint). Returns HTTP 503 with correlation ID for monitoring tracing.

checkFirestore() — checks Firestore connectivity by reading the admin_configuration/global document. Lightweight read that should complete in <100ms. Returns ServiceStatus with responseTime. Critical service: if Firestore is down all database operations fail.

checkAuth() — checks Firebase Auth connectivity by obtaining the Auth Admin SDK instance. Validates that the Firebase Admin SDK can connect to the Auth service. Critical service: if Auth is down users cannot login or signup.

Module header for /api/admin/email/health endpoint. Health check for the Microsoft Graph API / email interface. Admin-only (Firebase Auth token + isAdmin verified). Makes a lightweight test call to Microsoft Graph to verify credentials are valid and connectivity is good.

GET handler for /api/admin/email/health. Verifies admin auth token, checks GRAPH_* env vars are configured, acquires an OAuth2 access token from Microsoft, then calls GET /me on Graph API. Returns healthy:true with responseTime and sender email if all checks pass; healthy:false with detailed error hints on failure.

Module header for lib/validation.ts. Centralised Zod validation schemas for API request validation. Provides strict type safety and input sanitisation to prevent injection attacks and malformed requests across all API routes.

AdminHotLinkSchema — Zod schema for admin hot-link (magic link) token exchange requests. Validates token as exactly 64-character hex string (crypto.randomBytes(32)) and email as RFC 5322 compliant. Prevents token enumeration attacks and rejects malformed tokens before any database lookup.

AdminChallengeRequestSchema — Zod schema for admin challenge (magic link generation) requests. Minimal validation as admin status is verified server-side via Firebase Auth token. Includes optional _ipAddress field for server-side rate limiting.

EmailSchema — Zod schema for strict email validation. Rejects emails with special characters that could enable XSS or injection attacks. Enforces RFC 5322 compliance with 255-character max and character allowlist regex. Used across login, signup, and email verification routes.

generateSecureToken() — generates cryptographically secure hex tokens using Node.js crypto.randomBytes(). Replaces Math.random() usage (LIB-002 vulnerability). Used for admin challenge tokens and other security-critical token generation.

generateSecureCorrelationId() — generates cryptographically secure correlation IDs using crypto.randomUUID() (RFC 4122 v4). Fixes LIB-002 (predictable correlation IDs using Math.random). Ensures audit trail integrity and prevents correlation ID prediction attacks.

ADMIN_CHALLENGE_RATE_LIMITS — rate limit thresholds for admin challenge (magic link) operations. Limits: 3 per user per hour, 100 global per hour, 5-minute cooldown after successful challenge. Prevents email spam via magic link generation abuse and DoS via exhausting email quota.

Module header and League/CreateLeagueData/LeagueWithMemberCount interface definitions for the league system. Leagues use a lens architecture — they filter master standings data without duplicating it. Changing the League interface requires updates to Firestore read/write logic in leagues.ts and all UI components that destructure league documents.

League system constants: GLOBAL_LEAGUE_ID ('global'), SYSTEM_OWNER_ID ('system'), INVITE_CODE_LENGTH (6), MAX_LEAGUES_PER_USER (5 including global). These are the single source of truth for league business rules. Changing GLOBAL_LEAGUE_ID or SYSTEM_OWNER_ID requires a Firestore data migration. Changing MAX_LEAGUES_PER_USER affects join/create eligibility enforced in API routes.

Master race calendar for the 2026 F1 season (24 races). Defines qualifying deadlines for client-side UX only. GEMINI-AUDIT-052 accepted risk: timing data is public (official F1 calendar) and server-side enforcement via Firestore (race-schedule-server.ts) is the authoritative deadline gate. BOW risk Cy4hqh5EXe53Ww2LkqLR: this static array can desync from the Firestore race_schedule collection — any admin calendar update requires BOTH a Firestore write AND a code deployment to keep client UX consistent. Known technical debt: components should be refactored to fetch from an authenticated API endpoint.

Module-level GUID for the acknowledge-attack API route. Server-side endpoint replacing direct client-side Firestore writes from AttackMonitor. Admin-only POST endpoint for acknowledging attack alerts stored in attack_alerts collection.

POST handler for attack alert acknowledgement. Authenticates user via Firebase Auth token, verifies admin status in Firestore, validates alertId/alertIds payload, checks all documents exist, then updates attack_alerts with acknowledged=true, acknowledgedBy, and acknowledgedAt.
Authentication step in POST handler — extracts Authorization header and calls verifyAuthToken. Returns 401 if token is invalid or missing.

Admin privilege check — reads users/{uid} document and verifies isAdmin flag. Returns 403 if user is not an admin. Note: v02 of this GUID also serves as the catch-block error handler (dual use of sequence number in this file).
Request body parsing and validation — supports single alertId (string) or batch alertIds (string[]). Returns 400 if neither field is present or correctly typed.
Pre-update existence check — fetches all attack_alerts documents by ID before any writes. Returns 404 if any alert ID does not exist, preventing partial updates.
Parallel update step — updates all validated alert documents with acknowledged=true, acknowledgedBy, and server timestamp. Real-time listeners in AttackMonitor reflect these changes immediately.

Module-level GUID for the resolve-error admin API route. Server-side endpoint replacing direct client-side Firestore writes from ErrorLogViewer. Admin-only POST to mark error_logs documents as resolved.
POST handler — authenticates caller, verifies admin status, validates errorLogId, checks document exists, and marks it as resolved with resolvedAt ISO timestamp and resolvedBy admin UID.
Authentication step — extracts Authorization header and calls verifyAuthToken. Returns 401 if token is invalid or missing.
Admin privilege check — reads users/{uid} document and verifies isAdmin flag. Returns 403 if user is not an admin.
Request body validation — parses JSON body and validates errorLogId is a non-empty string. Returns 400 if missing or wrong type.
Document existence check — fetches error_logs/{errorLogId} before attempting update. Returns 404 if the document does not exist.
Resolution write — updates error_logs/{errorLogId} with resolved=true, resolvedAt ISO timestamp, and resolvedBy admin UID. Real-time listeners in ErrorLogViewer reflect the change.

Top-level catch block — creates traced error via createTracedError/logTracedError, logs to error_logs, returns safe 500 response with correlation ID. v02 upgraded from direct db.collection add to traced error infrastructure.

Module-level GUID for the unlock-account admin API route. Emergency unlock for locked-out users. Verifies admin auth, validates target user, resets badLoginAttempts to 0, clears lastFailedLoginAt, and writes audit_logs entry.
TypeScript interface for unlock-account request payload. Requires adminUid (matched against verified token UID) and targetUserEmail (identifies which account to unlock).

Main POST handler — verifies Firebase Auth token, confirms token UID matches submitted adminUid, checks admin privileges in Firestore, queries user by normalised email, resets badLoginAttempts to 0, deletes lastFailedLoginAt field, and writes audit_logs entry.
Catch block error handler — creates traced error with UNKNOWN_ERROR code and logs via logTracedError. Returns safe 500 response with correlation ID.

Module-level GUID for the verify-access admin API route. Validates admin magic-link tokens: constant-time comparison (crypto.timingSafeEqual), re-use check, expiry check, admin privilege re-check, then sets httpOnly adminVerified cookie.

POST handler — full admin verification flow: field validation, token document fetch, constant-time comparison, re-use and expiry checks, admin privileges re-check, mark-as-used write, audit log write, and httpOnly cookie set.
Missing fields check — validates both token and uid are present in request body. Returns 400 with VALIDATION_MISSING_FIELDS error code if either is absent.
Token validation block — fetches admin_verification_tokens/{uid}, performs crypto.timingSafeEqual comparison to prevent timing attacks, checks used flag, and checks expiry against current time.
Post-token admin privilege re-check — verifies users/{uid} still has isAdmin=true. Returns 403 if admin privileges have been revoked since the token was issued.
Token invalidation — marks admin_verification_tokens/{uid} as used=true with server timestamp. Prevents replay attacks by ensuring each token can only be used once.
Audit log write — records ADMIN_VERIFICATION_SUCCESS to audit_logs collection with admin UID, email, IP address, and user-agent for accountability.

httpOnly cookie set — sets adminVerified=true as a 7-day httpOnly, secure, SameSite=lax cookie. httpOnly prevents XSS-based bypass. Server Component reads cookie to gate admin panel access. v04 reflects current stable implementation.

Top-level catch block — logs uncaught exceptions via createTracedError/logTracedError. No raw error.message exposed in response (security compliance). Returns correlation ID for support tracking. v02 replaced direct error_logs write with traced error infrastructure.

Module-level GUID for the admin WhatsApp health-check route. Admin-only GET endpoint checking connectivity to the WhatsApp worker container. v02 added isAdmin Firestore check to prevent non-admin users from probing the internal worker URL.

GET handler — verifies Firebase Auth token, checks isAdmin in Firestore, validates WHATSAPP_WORKER_URL env var, fetches /health on worker with 5s timeout, returns JSON with healthy flag, response time, and status code. v02 added isAdmin Firestore check.
Module-level GUID for the admin OpenF1 drivers route. Fetches driver list from OpenF1 API for a given session key to populate driver filter dropdowns in PubChatPanel. Admin-only.

OpenF1 OAuth2 token acquisition with in-memory caching (55-minute TTL). Reads OPENF1_USERNAME/PASSWORD from env, posts to OpenF1 token endpoint, caches access_token. Returns null if credentials missing or token fetch fails.

GET handler — verifies Firebase Auth token, checks isAdmin in Firestore, validates sessionKey query param, fetches drivers from OpenF1 /drivers with OAuth2 Bearer token, deduplicates by driver_number, returns sorted driver list.
OpenF1 API call and deduplication — fetches drivers for sessionKey, deduplicates by driver_number using a Map, formats as {number, name} objects, sorts ascending by driver number.
Top-level catch block — creates traced error with OPENF1_FETCH_FAILED code, logs via logTracedError, returns safe 500 response with correlation ID.

Module-level GUID for the admin OpenF1 location route. Server-side proxy for real-time car GPS position data from OpenF1 /location. Handles OAuth2 auth and prevents client-side CORS issues. Note: only verifies auth token, no isAdmin check — unlike most other admin routes.

OpenF1 OAuth2 token acquisition with in-memory caching (55-minute TTL). Uses getSecret() from secrets-manager for OPENF1_USERNAME and OPENF1_PASSWORD. Returns null if credentials unavailable or token fetch fails.

GET handler — verifies Firebase Auth token, checks isAdmin Firestore flag (security fix v02: was missing, matched openf1-drivers pattern), validates sessionKey as numeric param, acquires OpenF1 OAuth2 token, fetches /location data, validates array response format, and returns position records array with count.

Module-level GUID for the admin update-hot-news route. Server-side endpoint replacing direct client-side Firestore writes from HotNewsManager. Admin-only POST to update hot news content and toggle state in app-settings/hot-news. Enforces 10,000 character content limit.
TypeScript interface for the update-hot-news request payload. Requires adminUid, content (string), and hotNewsFeedEnabled (boolean).

Main POST handler — verifies Firebase Auth token, validates input types and presence, enforces 10,000 character content limit, checks UID match between token and adminUid, verifies isAdmin flag, updates app-settings/hot-news via merge set, and writes audit_logs entry. v02 added input validation security fix.
Top-level catch block — creates traced error with UNKNOWN_ERROR code, logs via logTracedError, returns safe 500 response with correlation ID.

Comprehensive client-side error logger component. Captures all unhandled window errors and promise rejections, forwards them to /api/log-client-error. Skips chunk errors (handled separately by ChunkErrorHandler) and Firebase Performance attribute noise (PERF-001). Production-safe: console.error redacts stack/context/error object to prevent DevTools disclosure (GEMINI-AUDIT-044).

FeedbackForm — client-side form component for submitting bug reports and feature requests. Writes feedback documents to Firestore via runTransaction with optional notification-on-fix flag. Displays error details with correlation IDs. Uses CLIENT_ERRORS from error-registry-client.

API route module header for admin verification magic-link generation. Generates a CSPRNG 32-byte hex token, stores in admin_verification_tokens/{uid} with 30-minute expiry, sends magic link email. Called by admin page to request email-based challenge before accessing admin panel.

Radix UI Tooltip primitive wrappers. Exports TooltipProvider, Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, and TooltipContent with Tailwind-styled popover content. Used by JargonTip inline helper to display F1 terminology definitions on hover.
generateAdminVerificationToken() — generates a cryptographically secure 32-byte hex token via crypto.randomBytes(). Used as the admin magic-link verification token stored in Firestore.

POST handler — authenticates user via Firebase ID token, verifies isAdmin flag in Firestore, generates verification token with 30-minute expiry, stores in admin_verification_tokens/{uid}, sends magic link email, writes audit log.
Module-level fileOverview for hot-news-feed.ts — AI-generated race bulletin using live weather; defines flow architecture and write contract.
Zod output schema for hot news feed — newsFeed string, optional lastUpdated ISO timestamp, optional refreshCount.
Hardcoded lat/lng coordinates for all 24 F1 race venues to support Open-Meteo weather lookups without an API key.
WMO weather code to human-readable description map for converting Open-Meteo numeric codes into readable text for the AI prompt.
getHotNewsFeed() — reads app-settings/hot-news from Firestore via Admin SDK; returns content, lastUpdated, and refreshCount for the dashboard component.
getNextRace() helper — finds the next upcoming race from RaceSchedule by qualifying time; used to target weather fetches at the correct venue.
fetchOpenMeteoWeather() — fetches 3-day weather forecast from Open-Meteo (free, no key) for a given lat/lng; returns structured weather object or null on failure.
fetchOpenF1Weather() — opportunistically fetches live track weather from OpenF1 latest session; returns null if session is older than 6h or on any error.

hotNewsFeedFlow — Genkit AI flow: fetches weather, builds prompt, calls Gemini 2.0 Flash, writes bulletin + refreshCount to app-settings/hot-news. Appends #NNNN messageId suffix to generated text (starts at #0018).
Module-level fileOverview for /api/cron/refresh-hot-news route — cron-authenticated POST endpoint that triggers AI hot news regeneration hourly.
isAuthorized() — timing-safe bearer token validation against CRON_SECRET env var; prevents token oracle attacks on the cron endpoint.
POST handler for /api/cron/refresh-hot-news — validates bearer token, calls hotNewsFeedFlow(), returns { success, refreshCount }.
refreshHotNews Cloud Function — hourly onSchedule trigger that HTTP POSTs to /api/cron/refresh-hot-news with CRON_SECRET to regenerate the AI hot news bulletin.
AppEvents interface — typed event contract for all Firebase error signals; adding an event here widens the emitter type signature.
Generic strongly-typed pub/sub factory returning an emitter with on/off/emit methods constrained to the event map type T.
Module-level singleton errorEmitter typed with AppEvents — the single hub through which all Firestore permission errors propagate to FirebaseErrorListener.
Internal type definitions (SecurityRuleContext, FirebaseAuthToken, FirebaseAuthObject, SecurityRuleRequest) modelling Firestore security rule objects for LLM-friendly error formatting.
Converts a Firebase User into a FirebaseAuthObject mirroring request.auth in Firestore security rules — used to build diagnostic error payloads.
Assembles the full simulated SecurityRuleRequest by combining operation context with current auth state; safe against uninitialized Firebase app.
Formats the security rule request object into a human/LLM-readable string matching the Firestore "Missing or insufficient permissions" error format.
FirestorePermissionError — custom error class wrapping Firestore permission failures with a structured SecurityRuleRequest payload designed for LLM-assisted debugging.
Fire-and-forget anonymous sign-in — deliberately not awaited so the UI does not freeze; auth state change propagates via onAuthStateChanged listener.
Fire-and-forget email/password account creation — not awaited; result arrives via the onAuthStateChanged listener.
Fire-and-forget email/password sign-in — not awaited; auth state change propagates via onAuthStateChanged listener.
Fire-and-forget setDoc wrapper — writes the document without blocking; emits FirestorePermissionError via errorEmitter on permission failure.
Fire-and-forget addDoc wrapper — creates a new document without blocking; has explicit cascade-prevention for error_logs to avoid infinite error loops (skipErrorEmit flag).
Fire-and-forget updateDoc wrapper — merges fields into an existing document without blocking; emits permission-error on failure.
Fire-and-forget deleteDoc wrapper — removes a document without blocking; emits permission-error on failure.
WhatsApp alert schema interfaces (WhatsAppAlertToggles, WhatsAppAlertSettings, WhatsAppAlertHistoryEntry) — mirrors the admin_configuration/whatsapp_alerts Firestore document structure.
Reads WhatsApp alert settings from admin_configuration/whatsapp_alerts; creates the document with defaults if absent.
Merges partial WhatsApp alert settings into admin_configuration/whatsapp_alerts — used by the admin panel to toggle individual alert types.
Builds a Firestore Query for whatsapp_alert_history ordered by createdAt descending — returns a Query for real-time listener attachment by the admin history table.
Appends a new entry to whatsapp_alert_history, recording alert type, message, target group, status, and timestamps for send/fail audit trail.
PubChat settings interface, defaults, reader (getPubChatSettings), and writer (updatePubChatContent) — manages the app-settings/pub-chat admin-editable announcement text.
PubChatTimingDriver and PubChatTimingData TypeScript interfaces defining the shape of the app-settings/pub-chat-timing Firestore document sourced from OpenF1 API.
Reads session timing data from app-settings/pub-chat-timing; returns null if absent; supports forceServer flag to bypass cache after an admin OpenF1 fetch.
React hook that builds a copyable error string (errorCode | correlationId | digest | moduleName) with a 2-second copied confirmation — implements Golden Rule #1 selectable-error requirement.
React hook returning true when viewport width is below the 768px mobile breakpoint; updates reactively via MediaQueryList change events.

Module-level toast notification state machine with reducer, listener array, and useToast hook — provides the imperative toast() API and the Toaster component's data source. TOAST_LIMIT=1 enforces single-toast display.
Invisible component subscribed to errorEmitter's permission-error event; logs errors to the audit trail and throws them into React's error boundary for Next.js global-error.tsx display.
Renders the Prix Six SVG logo at sm/md/lg size with priority loading to prevent layout shift on auth and branded pages.
IS_PRE_SEASON flag and amber alert banner informing players that test-season points will reset before race 1; returns null immediately when IS_PRE_SEASON=false.

The Paddock Pub Chat widget with three view modes: leaderboard (ranked timing), team-lens (official driver roster joined with OpenF1 timing), and comparison (multi-select driver delta). Falls back to pre-season test data when Firestore timing is absent.
Genkit dev entry point — loads env vars and imports all AI flow modules so the Genkit dev server discovers them for local testing. Not loaded in production.
Genkit flow with placeholder scrapeF1DriversTool — intended to scrape F1.com for the current driver list and update Firestore; implementation is a TODO stub with deferred GR#7 violations.
Genkit flow that uses a Vertex AI prompt to generate a punny F1 team name based on an existing real team name — utility flow for the team name selection feature.
Admin magic-link verification page — extracts token and uid from URL params, POSTs to /api/admin/verify-access, and redirects to /admin on success. Single-use, 10-minute expiry.
Server component fetching AI-generated Hot News Feed content and rendering it as a card with timestamp and refresh counter; also exports HotNewsFeedSkeleton for Suspense boundaries.
One-time dashboard notification for resolved feedback items — queries feedback collection, shows green alerts for resolved but unnotified items, sets resolvedNotifiedAt on dismiss.
League dropdown selector — only renders when user belongs to more than one league; reads/writes selected league via LeagueContext to drive league-filtered views.

TypeScript types and interfaces for the centralized Book of Work system: BookOfWorkCategory, BookOfWorkStatus, BookOfWorkSeverity, BookOfWorkSource, BookOfWorkPackage enums and BookOfWorkEntry/Stats interfaces.
BookOfWorkCategory type — defines all possible categories for work items: security, ui, feature, cosmetic, infrastructure, system-error, user-error.

BookOfWorkEntryCreate helper type — BookOfWorkEntry without id/timestamps (optional) for use with addDoc() and migration scripts with historical dates.
BookOfWorkEntryUpdate helper type — partial BookOfWorkEntry with required updatedAt for use with updateDoc() partial updates in the admin UI.

Enforce 5-minute cooldown between runs to prevent Denial of Wallet via repeated unbounded Firestore reads (GEMINI-AUDIT-017).
Live countdown timer — updates cooldownRemaining every second while cooldown is active.
Sanitize stored email HTML from Firestore before rendering in the admin preview pane. Uses a strict allowlist of safe tags/attrs sufficient for email body display.

Hourly cron job to delete expired admin challenge tokens from the admin_challenges collection. Provides defensive redundancy alongside Firestore TTL policy (Option C approach). Prevents token accumulation and ensures tokens older than 10 minutes are purged.

Firestore security rules for admin_challenges collection (Admin Hot Link tokens). Denies all client-side access. Tokens are created and verified server-side only via /api/auth/admin-challenge and /api/admin/verify-access endpoints.

Admin component for real-time health monitoring of external interfaces (PubChat/OpenF1, WhatsApp, Email/Graph API). Provides RAG (Red/Amber/Green) status indicators with diagnostic information to quickly identify interface failures.
InterfaceHealthMonitor component — displays real-time health status for PubChat, WhatsApp, and Email interfaces with RAG indicators and diagnostic info.
Check PubChat/OpenF1 interface health by calling the sessions API endpoint.
Check WhatsApp worker interface health by calling the health endpoint.
Check Email/Graph API interface health by calling the health endpoint.
Run all health checks in parallel and update lastFullCheck timestamp.
Firestore security rules for secondary_email_verification_tokens collection. Denies all client-side access. Tokens created server-side only.

Server-side account closure endpoint — permanently deletes a user's Firestore document, predictions subcollection, presence document, and Firebase Auth account using Admin SDK. Client-side Firestore rules deny user-document deletes; this route bypasses them safely.
DELETE_SUBCOLLECTION_BATCH_SIZE controls how many prediction documents are deleted per Firestore batch write. Firestore enforces a hard limit of 500 operations per batch.

POST handler — verifies the caller's Firebase ID token, then atomically deletes: 1. All documents in users/{uid}/predictions subcollection (batched) 2. The users/{uid} Firestore document 3. The presence/{uid} Firestore document (best-effort, non-fatal if missing) 4. The Firebase Auth account record for uid Writes an audit log entry before deletion completes.
Authenticate the request via the Authorization: Bearer <token> header. Returns 401 immediately for missing, expired, or invalid tokens.

Delete all documents in users/{uid}/predictions subcollection using batched writes. Subcollection documents are NOT automatically deleted when the parent document is deleted, so explicit deletion is required to prevent orphaned prediction data.

Write a final audit log entry recording the account closure event before the user document is deleted. This ensures an audit trail exists even after the account is gone. Written directly via Admin SDK since the client-side audit module is unavailable here.
Delete the users/{uid} Firestore document via Admin SDK. Admin SDK bypasses the Firestore security rules that deny client-side deletes on user documents.
Delete the presence/{uid} document. Presence documents track online status and are separate from the users collection. Best-effort: a missing presence doc is not an error.

Delete the Firebase Auth account for uid using the Admin Auth SDK. This is the final step so that re-authentication is impossible after all Firestore data is removed. If this step fails, the Firestore data is already deleted — the user has no document to sign in to, and the auth record can be cleaned up manually by an admin.

Top-level catch for any unhandled error during the deletion pipeline. Logs to error_logs via logError (Golden Rule #1) and returns a structured error response with correlation ID so the user can report the exact failure to support.

Generic helper to fetch any OpenF1 data type for a session, with an optional driver filter. Builds the URL from dataType and sessionKey, then calls fetchWithTimeout() and safeParseJson() to guard against hangs and non-JSON responses.

When validate=true, fire a parallel /laps?session_key=X&limit=1 check for each past session to determine whether OpenF1 actually has lap data. Future sessions are unconditionally hasData=false. Returns hasData field on each session so the client can suppress sessions with no data without the user seeing empty fetches.
Authenticate and authorise the request. Only admin users may read the aggregate prediction count. This replaces the previous unauthenticated client-side collectionGroup read.

Fetch prediction documents server-side with a hard cap (PREDICTION_FETCH_CAP) to prevent unbounded reads. Count unique team entries (primary and secondary) by building a Set of "{userId}_{teamName}" keys.
Top-level error handler — catches any unhandled exception, logs with correlation ID, and returns a safe 500 with correlationId for support tracing (Golden Rule #1 compliance).
Authenticate the user via Authorization header.
Verify the authenticated user has admin privileges.
Generate verification token and store in Firestore with 30-minute expiry.
Build admin verification URL with encoded parameters.
Send verification email with magic link button.
Send the email via Graph API.
Log successful admin verification request to audit log.
Validate that the request originates from an allowed domain to prevent CSRF attacks.
Find ALL user predictions for this race across all user subcollections, handling both raceId storage formats (raw with -GP suffix and normalized without).
Remove all user predictions for the race to prevent orphaned data.

Limit the number of pending emails fetched per push to prevent memory exhaustion and execution-time timeout. With ~1s per email (Graph API call), 50 emails ≈ 50s — within the Next.js 60s default timeout. Admins can push multiple times to drain a larger queue. hasMore=true in the push response signals that another push is required.

Module-level timestamp captured at Cloud Run instance startup. Used to detect cold starts: if this request arrives within 30s of instance load, it IS the cold start. Module-level variables persist for the lifetime of the Cloud Run instance (warm = reused).

Log cold-start event to Firestore once per instance lifetime. Uses module-level flag to prevent repeated writes on subsequent warm requests. Fire-and-forget (no await) — never block the health response for logging.

Server-side API route for deleting a league. Uses Admin SDK to bypass Firestore security rules, enforcing ownership or admin privilege server-side. Resolves GEMINI-002 breakage where Firestore rules restricted client-side delete to admin-only, leaving non-admin owners unable to delete their own leagues.
POST handler — authenticates user, validates leagueId format, verifies league ownership or admin status atomically via Firestore transaction, then deletes + audits in one operation.
Authenticate the requesting user via Firebase ID token.

Validate request body — leagueId must match Firestore ID character set. Rejects path traversal chars (slashes, dots) before any SDK call. @RED_TEAM (Wave 13): Added LEAGUE_ID_REGEX to prevent path traversal via slash/dot in leagueId.
Check user admin status before entering transaction (isAdmin field is protected by Firestore rules, so TOCTOU risk for this read is negligible).

Atomically read league, enforce all business rules, delete league, and write audit log using a Firestore transaction. Eliminates TOCTOU races on isGlobal and ownerId. @RED_TEAM (Wave 13): runTransaction replaces batch — re-reads league inside transaction so any isGlobal or ownerId change between validation and deletion is caught atomically. Audit log write is inside the same transaction (not a sequential await) for guaranteed atomicity.

Double global guard — checks both the constant ID and the isGlobal field. Either condition alone is sufficient to block deletion of the global league. @RED_TEAM (Wave 13): Double guard prevents deletion even if isGlobal is temporarily flipped to false by admin error. Both checks execute inside the transaction.
Rate limit configuration — max 10 join attempts per IP per 15-minute window. Chosen to allow legitimate retries (mistyped codes) while blocking enumeration.
Derive the originating client IP from request headers in priority order.
Check whether the given IP has exceeded JOIN_RATE_LIMIT_MAX attempts within the current 15-minute window. Returns false on any read failure (fail-open for availability).
Increment attempt counter for this IP. Resets window if expired or doc missing. Write failures are logged but non-blocking — one missed count is acceptable.
Enforce max 10 join attempts per IP per 15-minute window. Returns HTTP 429 if exceeded, before any league data is accessed.

Firestore security rules for the audit_logs collection (FIRESTORE-007). Client-side audit writes are permitted for navigation and UI events where server round-trips are impractical. Update and delete are blocked unconditionally — audit records are immutable. This is the correct pattern: not a security gap.

Prix Six Cloud Functions — Backup & Recovery. Automated daily Firestore backups to GCS plus weekly Sunday smoke test that restores backup into a disposable recovery project.
Initialise the Firebase Admin SDK once at cold-start so all downstream function handlers can call getFirestore()/getAuth().
Centralise all environment-specific constants. Added project isolation guard (Wave 10) — throws at cold-start if RECOVERY_PROJECT equals MAIN_PROJECT to prevent accidental import into production.

Server-side Firebase Admin SDK initialisation and shared utilities for authentication verification, correlation ID generation, and centralised error logging. Security fix: credential leak in initialization error catch block.
Module-level singleton cache for the Firebase Admin SDK app, Firestore instance, FieldValue, and Timestamp to avoid re-initialisation on every request.
getFirebaseAdmin function — initialises or returns the Admin SDK singleton. Returns db, FieldValue, and Timestamp instances.
Firestore security rules for the book-of-work collection. Controls read/write access to the papers BOW tracking collection.
Predictions page — allows users to view, submit, and edit driver predictions for the next open race. Supports multiple teams, prediction carry-over, and pit lane open/closed status.
Schedule page — displays the full 2026 F1 race calendar with qualifying, sprint, and GP times in the user local timezone. Highlights next upcoming race, dims past races.
Converts an ISO 8601 date string into a localised date and time pair for display. Called per race event (qualifying, sprint, GP) during the calendar rendering loop.
Signup page — registration is CLOSED. Renders invitation-only card and links back to login. No Firebase SDK calls to avoid IndexedDB storms on Safari iOS (BUG-ERR-003 root cause).
Static server component — no client JS, no Firebase SDK, no IndexedDB. Renders registration closed message with a link to /login.

Admin pit lane override gate in the submit-prediction handler. Checks app-settings/pit-lane.override before the time-based qualifying lock — force-close rejects immediately, force-open bypasses the clock check while race results check still applies.

Scheduled Cloud Function (2nd-gen, Cloud Run) that fires every 15 minutes via Cloud Scheduler and POSTs to /api/cron/process-email-queue. A thin HTTP trigger — all email draining logic lives in the app route.

Lazy synchronous-style accessors (adminAuth, adminFirestore) for legacy routes that import them as named exports. Uses getFirebaseAdmin() internally to ensure Admin SDK is initialized. New code should prefer getFirebaseAdmin() directly.

Firestore security rule granting read access to official_teams collection for signed-in users. official_teams contains public F1 reference data (team names, driver numbers, colours) used by PubChatPanel Team Lens. Write restricted to admins.

Subscribes to the app-settings/pit-lane Firestore document to get the admin override state (null = automatic clock-based, open = force open, close = force close). Used to compute the effective isPitlaneOpen value on the predictions page.
On mount, scrolls the next race card into view so it appears at the top of the schedule list. Past races sit above, future races below. Uses instant scroll behaviour to avoid a jarring animated jump.

Auto-fetch guard in PubChatPanel: when the initial Firestore read completes and there is no timing data, automatically calls /api/live/refresh-timing to pull the latest OpenF1 session — so the admin never needs to manually trigger the first fetch. A ref prevents re-firing on subsequent renders.

Auto-selects the most relevant session when sessions load for a selected meeting. Priority: (1) session in progress, (2) next upcoming session, (3) most recently completed. Prevents stale pre-season data from defaulting — admin lands on the correct session immediately (BUG-PC-002 fix).
UpcomingSession interface — shape of a single session entry produced by getUpcomingSessions. Includes label, raceName, location, time, approx flag, locksPicks flag, and isSprint flag.
Build list of next N upcoming F1 sessions by expanding RaceSchedule into individual sessions. Normal weekends: FP1/FP2/FP3/Q/GP. Sprint weekends: FP1/SQ/S/Q/GP. Filters to future only.
Module-level fileOverview for /api/cron/sync-session-times route — daily cron POST that syncs OpenF1 session start/end times into race_schedule Firestore collection.
isAuthorized — validates CRON_SECRET bearer token with timing-safe comparison before OpenF1/Firestore access.
OpenF1Session interface — shape of session objects from OpenF1 /v1/sessions API used by the sync route.
POST handler — fetches OpenF1 sessions for next 30 days, matches to race_schedule docs, batch-writes qualifyingTime/sprintTime/raceTime/raceEndTime updates.
effectiveCutoff computation — reads app-settings/pit-lane and race_schedule to determine the actual scoring deadline, using overriddenAt if admin extended the window past scheduled qualifying.
syncSessionTimes — daily 05:00 UTC Cloud Function that POSTs to /api/cron/sync-session-times to sync OpenF1 session times into race_schedule Firestore collection.
nextMilestone computation — walks qualifying→sprint→race within active race weekend to find next upcoming session. Falls back to next race qualifying when all sessions are past.
pitLaneClosedAt + lockedSessions computation — derives actual close timestamp from overriddenAt or scheduled qualifyingTime, builds locked sessions label for the pit lane closed card.

tree.description: Codebase folder map — describes the purpose of every directory. Maintained alongside code.json. Last updated: 2026-02-24.

..description: Project root for Prix Six. Contains Firebase config (firebase.json, firestore.indexes.json, .firebaserc), npm workspace root (package.json), the module registry (code.json), project documentation (claude.md, SECRETS-ARCHITECTURE.md, SECURITY.md), and the four Claude Code coordination hooks (claude-sync.js, claude-startup.js, claude-ping-check.js, claude-statusline.js). All other code lives in sub-folders.
key_files: claude.md — Claude Code project rules (Golden Rules, session protocol, GUID docs)
claude-sync.js — Multi-session permit/lease coordination via Firestore (DHCP model)
claude-startup.js — Claude Code SessionStart hook
claude-ping-check.js — Claude Code PostToolUse hook (permit auto-renewal)
claude-statusline.js — Claude Code status line renderer
code.json — Module GUID registry (1000+ entries, source of truth for all modules)
book-of-work.json — Outstanding work items tracker (local mirror of Firestore book_of_work)
firebase.json — Firebase hosting + Functions deployment config
firestore.indexes.json — Firestore composite index definitions
service-account.json — Firebase Admin SDK credentials (gitignored, never commit)
SECRETS-ARCHITECTURE.md — Dual-vault credential map (Azure Key Vault + Firebase Secret Manager)
RedTeam.json — Security audit findings from Gemini Red Team exercise

.claude.description: Claude Code local configuration for this project. Contains settings.json which registers the four hook scripts (SessionStart, PostToolUse, PreCompact, statusLine), and runtime state files written by the hooks at session time (.last-ping, .session-map).
key_files: settings.json — Hook registrations: claude-startup.js, claude-ping-check.js, claude-statusline.js

.github.description: GitHub Actions CI/CD configuration. Contains workflow YAML files for automated build, test, and deployment pipelines.
key_files: workflows/ — GitHub Actions workflow definitions

.github/workflows.description: GitHub Actions workflow YAML files. Define automated pipelines triggered on push/PR events — typically build validation and Firebase deployment.

_Share.description: Windows shortcut link folder. Contains a shared.lnk shortcut pointing to a shared drive or OneDrive location used for distributing build artifacts or design assets to the team. Not part of the application build.

shared.description: Shared code and schemas used across multiple sub-projects (app, functions, whatsapp-worker). Currently contains error-codes.json — a cross-project error code registry that ensures consistent error identifiers between the Next.js app, Cloud Functions, and the WhatsApp worker.
key_files: error-codes.json — Cross-project error code definitions

docs.description: High-Level Design (HLD) and architecture documentation. Contains ER diagrams, the complete HLD document in multiple versions (MD and DOCX), and backup monitoring runbooks. These are design-time documents, not referenced at runtime.
key_files: PRIX_SIX_HLD_COMPLETE_V4.md — Latest HLD (authoritative design reference)
PRIX_SIX_ER_DIAGRAM.md — Entity Relationship diagram for all Firestore collections
backup-monitoring.md — Runbook for Firebase backup monitoring procedures

app.description: Next.js 14 application root. The main production application — a fantasy F1 prediction league called Prix Six. Contains all front-end pages, API routes, shared components, Firebase integration, scoring engine, and email system. Built with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ShadCN UI, and Firebase (Auth + Firestore).
key_files: package.json — App dependencies and build scripts
src/ — All application source code
public/ — Static assets served at the web root
email/ — Transactional email templates

app/src.description: Root of all application TypeScript/TSX source files. Structured following Next.js App Router conventions with route groups, API handlers, components, contexts, hooks, and libraries.

app/src/app.description: Next.js App Router root. Contains route groups: (app) for authenticated pages, (auth) for login/signup flows, and api/ for server-side API handlers. The layout.tsx and error.tsx at this level are the application shell.

app/src/app/(app).description: All authenticated user-facing page routes. Protected by the session context — unauthenticated requests are redirected to /login. Each sub-folder is a route segment rendered within the main authenticated layout.

routes.dashboard: Home dashboard — shows upcoming race, user's prediction status, and league summary
routes.predictions: Race prediction submission form — allows users to rank their top-6 drivers before pit-lane lockout
routes.predictions/_components: Sub-components for the prediction page (driver selector, drag-and-drop ranking)
routes.standings: Season standings table — cumulative league points per team with race-by-race breakdown chart
routes.results: Race results page — shows actual finishing order vs user predictions with point breakdown
routes.my-results: Personal results history — user's scoring history across all races
routes.schedule: Season race calendar — upcoming and completed race weekends with lockout countdowns
routes.teams: Team directory — lists all registered teams in the league
routes.leagues: League management — join, view, and manage private leagues
routes.leagues/[leagueId]: Dynamic league detail page — standings filtered to league members
routes.profile: User profile — team name, email, notification preferences
routes.onboarding: New user onboarding flow — team name setup and prediction tutorial
routes.submissions: Submission history — all predictions a user has ever submitted
routes.rules: Scoring rules page — explains the Prix Six hybrid scoring model
routes.about: About page — app version, credits, and feature overview
routes.about/dev: Developer info page — build metadata, GUID count, environment info
routes.admin: Admin panel — restricted to isAdmin=true users
routes.admin/_components: Admin panel sub-components (ResultsManager, ScoringManager, TeamManager, etc.)
routes.admin/verify: Admin verification challenge page — secondary auth for sensitive admin actions
routes.audit: Security audit log viewer — admin-only view of audit_logs collection

app/src/app/(auth).description: Authentication flow routes — public pages (no auth required). Handles login (PIN + OAuth), signup, email verification, PIN reset, and profile completion after OAuth.

routes.login: PIN and Google/Apple OAuth login page
routes.signup: New account registration with team name and email
routes.forgot-pin: PIN reset flow via email verification
routes.complete-profile: Post-OAuth profile completion (set team name, PIN)

app/src/app/api.description: Next.js API route handlers — server-side endpoints. All routes are POST unless noted. Admin routes require isAdmin=true (verified server-side). Auth routes are public. Scoring routes are admin-protected.

routes.calculate-scores: POST — Admin: calculate and persist race scores from submitted results
routes.delete-scores: POST — Admin: cascade-delete scores, predictions, and race_results for a race
routes.submit-prediction: POST — User: submit or update race prediction (enforces pit-lane lockout)
routes.health: GET — Public health check endpoint for uptime monitoring
routes.email-queue: POST — Queue a transactional email for async sending
routes.log-client-error: POST — Log client-side error to error_logs collection
routes.auth: Auth sub-routes (login, signup, reset-pin, verify flows, OAuth completion)
routes.admin: Admin sub-routes (user management, OpenF1 data, WhatsApp health, site config)
routes.ai/analysis: POST — AI-powered prediction analysis using Genkit/Gemini
routes.send-*: Transactional email sending routes (results, welcome, PIN, verification, etc.)
routes.add-secondary-team: POST — Add a secondary team to an existing user account
routes.update-secondary-email: POST — Update secondary team email address
routes.verify-secondary-email: POST — Verify secondary team email token
routes.whatsapp-proxy: POST — Proxy endpoint for WhatsApp message routing

app/src/components.description: Reusable React components shared across multiple pages. Organised by domain: ui/ for primitive ShadCN components, layout/ for page structure, league/ for league-specific UI, icons/ for custom SVG icons.

app/src/components/ui.description: ShadCN UI primitive components (Button, Card, Table, Badge, Dialog, Toast, etc.) plus custom extensions (LastUpdated, Progress). These are the atomic building blocks used across all pages.

app/src/components/layout.description: Page layout components — navigation bar, sidebar, footer, and the authenticated app shell wrapper. Consumed by the (app) layout.tsx.

app/src/components/league.description: League-specific UI components — LeagueSelector dropdown used on Standings and Results pages to filter data by league membership.

app/src/components/icons.description: Custom SVG icon components not available in lucide-react — F1 flag, driver helmet icons, and other domain-specific graphics.

app/src/contexts.description: React Context providers that share state across the component tree. LeagueContext provides the selected league filter globally. SessionContext manages auth state and user profile for the authenticated app shell.
key_files: league-context.tsx — Global league filter state (selectedLeague, setSelectedLeague)
session-context.tsx — Auth session and user profile (user, isLoading, signOut)

app/src/features.description: Feature-scoped modules that bundle related logic not cleanly belonging to a single page or library. Currently contains the auth feature module.

app/src/features/auth.description: Authentication feature module. Contains authService.ts — a high-level service layer wrapping Firebase Auth operations (PIN login, OAuth, email verification, token management) with 4-pillar error handling.
key_files: authService.ts — Firebase Auth service: signIn, signUp, resetPin, verifyEmail, OAuth flows

app/src/firebase.description: Firebase client SDK initialization and provider components. Initialises the Firebase app, Auth, and Firestore instances, and exports React hooks (useFirestore, useAuth) for consuming them. The provider.tsx wraps the app in FirebaseProvider.
key_files: config.ts — Firebase client config (reads from env vars)
index.ts — Re-exports useFirestore, useAuth hooks
provider.tsx — FirebaseProvider React component
server.ts — Server-side Firebase Admin wrapper
firestore/ — Firestore-specific client utilities

app/src/hooks.description: Custom React hooks. use-mobile.tsx detects mobile viewport. use-toast.ts manages toast notification state. use-error-copy.ts provides clipboard copy functionality for error correlation IDs.
key_files: use-mobile.tsx — Mobile breakpoint detection hook
use-toast.ts — Toast notification management
use-error-copy.ts — Error correlation ID clipboard copy

app/src/lib.description: Core application library — the heart of the backend logic. Contains the scoring engine, error registry, Firestore admin wrapper, auth helpers, email sending, data definitions, and all shared utilities. These modules are imported by API routes and server components.
key_files: scoring.ts — Score calculation, updateRaceScores, deleteRaceScores
scoring-rules.ts — Prix Six hybrid point values (exact/1-off/2-off/3+/bonus)
normalize-race-id.ts — Canonical race ID formatting (normalizeRaceId, generateRaceId)
data.ts — F1Drivers list and RaceSchedule (single source of truth for driver/race data)
error-registry.ts — Server-side error code registry (ERRORS.*)
error-registry-client.ts — Client-safe error registry (no internal metadata)
error-codes.ts — Error code generation and correlation ID utilities
traced-error.ts — 4-pillar traced error wrapper (log, type, correlationId, displayable)
firebase-admin.ts — Firebase Admin SDK initialisation and helper functions
email.ts — Transactional email sending (Resend API)
email-tracking.ts — Email delivery tracking and retry logic
leagues.ts — League CRUD operations and member management
scoring-rules.ts — Point values: exact=6, 1-off=4, 2-off=3, 3+=2, bonus=10
validation.ts — Input validation helpers
utils.ts — General utility functions
consistency.ts — Data consistency check helpers
csrf-protection.ts — CSRF token generation and validation
audit.ts — Audit log write helpers
secrets-manager.ts — Azure Key Vault + Firebase Secret Manager dual-vault accessor
version.ts — APP_VERSION constant (must match app/package.json)
commit-history.json — Deployment history with version numbers and dates

app/src/lib/types.description: Shared TypeScript type definitions used across lib/ modules. Keeps complex interface definitions out of implementation files.

app/src/services.description: External service integration layer. Currently contains authService.ts — a high-level Firebase Auth wrapper that provides error-handled authentication operations used by API routes.
key_files: authService.ts — High-level Firebase Auth service with 4-pillar error handling

app/src/types.description: Global TypeScript type declarations. errors.ts defines the application-wide error type interfaces used by the 4-pillar error handling system across all modules.
key_files: errors.ts — Application error type interfaces

app/src/ai.description: Genkit AI integration module. Wraps Google Genkit flows for AI-powered features: team name suggestions and prediction analysis using the Gemini model.
key_files: flows/ — Individual Genkit AI flow definitions

app/src/ai/flows.description: Individual Genkit AI flow definitions. Each flow is a self-contained AI operation: team-name-suggestions generates creative F1-themed team names, analysis flow provides AI commentary on prediction performance.

app/email.description: Transactional email system. Contains the email template engine and a test harness (email-test.js) for sending test emails during development. Templates are rendered server-side and sent via the Resend API.
key_files: email-test.js — Development utility for sending test emails (not part of app build)

app/public.description: Static assets served at the web root by Next.js. Contains logo.svg, robots.txt, and diagnostic.js (a client-side diagnostic script for production debugging).
key_files: logo.svg — Prix Six application logo
robots.txt — Search engine crawl directives
diagnostic.js — Client-side production diagnostic tool

app/api.description: Legacy root-level API folder (outside app/src). Contains admin/auth route stubs used during initial Firebase + Next.js integration testing. Superseded by app/src/app/api — kept for reference only.
key_files: admin/verify-access/ — Legacy admin verification route stub
auth/admin-challenge/ — Legacy admin challenge route stub

functions.description: Firebase Cloud Functions (Node.js). Currently contains a minimal index.js that exports the function entry points. Used for background tasks that cannot run in the Next.js serverless environment — e.g., scheduled jobs, Firestore triggers.
key_files: index.js — Cloud Functions entry point
package.json — Functions dependencies (deployed separately from app)

functions-python.description: Firebase Cloud Functions (Python). Contains the pub_chat_updater function — a Python Cloud Function that processes WhatsApp message events and updates the public chat Firestore collection.
key_files: pub_chat_updater/ — Python function: WhatsApp → Firestore pub_chat updater

functions-python/pub_chat_updater.description: Python Cloud Function that subscribes to WhatsApp message events and writes formatted messages to the pub_chat Firestore collection for display in the in-app public chat feature.

whatsapp-worker.description: Standalone Node.js WhatsApp bot worker. Runs as a persistent process (separate from the Next.js app) using whatsapp-web.js (Puppeteer-based). Connects to WhatsApp Web, receives messages, and forwards them to the app's /api/whatsapp-proxy endpoint. Session state is persisted in .wwebjs_auth/.
key_files: src/ — Worker source code
.wwebjs_auth/ — WhatsApp Web session persistence (browser profile, cookies)

whatsapp-worker/src.description: WhatsApp worker source files. firebase-config.ts and firebase-store.ts handle Firestore session storage. whatsapp-client.ts manages the WhatsApp connection. queue-processor.ts processes the incoming message queue. azure-store.ts integrates with Azure for credential retrieval.
key_files: index.ts — Worker entry point and process management
whatsapp-client.ts — WhatsApp Web connection and message handling
queue-processor.ts — Incoming message queue processor
firebase-store.ts — Firestore-backed session/state storage
azure-store.ts — Azure Key Vault credential retrieval for WhatsApp

scripts.description: All development, testing, and operational utility scripts. These scripts run OUTSIDE the production application — they connect directly to Firestore via the Admin SDK (service-account.json) and are used for data management, auditing, testing, and book-of-work management. NOT part of the Next.js build or deployment. Run from project root with: node scripts/<name>.js or npx ts-node scripts/<name>.ts

categories.Data (SCRIPT-DATA-*): Backup, restore, and purge Firestore collections (race_results, scores)
categories.Analysis (SCRIPT-ANALYZE-*): Query and analyse Firestore data, HAR captures, and backup files
categories.Check (SCRIPT-CHECK-*): Verify data integrity, error log state, and field correctness
categories.Validate (SCRIPT-VALIDATE-*): Cross-check remediation plan completion against source files
categories.BOW (SCRIPT-BOW-*): Query and manage the book-of-work (local JSON and Firestore)
categories.CodeJson (SCRIPT-CODEJSON-*): Maintain code.json GUID registry and module documentation
categories.Remediation (SCRIPT-REMEDIATE-*): Generate remediation plans from security audit findings
categories.Testing (SCRIPT-TEST-*): End-to-end Puppeteer tests and season simulation
categories.Utility (SCRIPT-UTIL-*): Environment provisioning and one-time setup tasks
categories.Deploy (SCRIPT-DEPLOY-*): Firebase index deployment and batch operation launchers
categories.TypeScript utilities (unlabelled): BOW management, audit, migration, and scoring scripts — pre-date the GUID system

key_scripts: SCRIPT-DATA-001 backup-race-results.js — Backup race_results to JSON
SCRIPT-DATA-002 restore-race-results.js — Restore race_results from JSON backup
SCRIPT-ANALYZE-002 analyze-firestore.js — Map Firestore read/write patterns from HAR
SCRIPT-CHECK-001 check-race-id-format.js — Audit raceId format consistency
SCRIPT-TEST-001 _simulate_season.js — Full season scoring simulation (no Firestore)
SCRIPT-TEST-002 puppeteer-test.js — Full-stack end-to-end Puppeteer QA test
SCRIPT-TEST-003 prix_six_engine.py — Python reference scoring engine
SCRIPT-DEPLOY-001 deploy-indexes.bat — Deploy Firestore indexes via CLI
score-season.ts — Score a full season from prediction data
audit-code-integrity.js — Cross-reference filesystem against code.json registry
populate-code-json/ — Multi-phase script suite to populate code.json GUID registry

scripts/populate-code-json.description: Multi-phase suite of TypeScript scripts that populated the code.json module registry. Phases 1–4 added timestamps, dependencies, call relationships, and calledBy mappings. Phase 6 validated completeness. Checkpoints folder holds JSON snapshots from each phase run. These scripts are now maintenance utilities — rerun if code.json falls significantly out of date.
key_files: apply-phase1.ts through apply-phase4.ts — Incremental registry population scripts
phase6-validate.ts — Post-population validation report generator
checkpoints/ — JSON snapshots saved at each phase completion
COMPLETION-REPORT.md — Final completion summary from the original population run

app-dev.description: Development workspace for features and designs in progress — NOT deployed to production. Contains three workstreams: Bug-Feature-Req (feature request and bug fix prototypes), Inbound-WhatsApp (WhatsApp inbound message handling prototypes), and Sub-Mission-Ana (submission analysis feature work-in-progress).
key_files: Bug-Feature-Req/ — Feature request prototypes and bug fix explorations
Inbound-WhatsApp/ — WhatsApp inbound message processing prototypes
Sub-Mission-Ana/ — Submission analysis UI and logic prototypes

app-dev/Bug-Feature-Req.description: Prototype components and scripts for new features and bug fixes not yet ready for the main app. Includes FeedbackManager, FeedbackForm, AdminTabs, team-name-generator, and hot-news-feed prototypes.

app-dev/Inbound-WhatsApp.description: Prototype code for handling inbound WhatsApp messages — routing, parsing, and response logic. Work in progress for the WhatsApp-based prediction submission feature.

app-dev/Sub-Mission-Ana.description: Prototype submission analysis sub-system (nicknamed 'Ana'). Explores AI-assisted prediction analysis and submission scoring insights beyond the main results page.

archived-book-of-works.description: Archived historical versions of book-of-work.json. Contains snapshots taken at key project milestones before major BOW restructuring. Read-only reference — the live BOW is book-of-work.json in the project root and the Firestore book_of_work collection.

blackstart.description: Black-start recovery environment. Contains a minimal .env.local, a stripped service-account.json, AARON-CHECKLIST.txt, and Bertie onboarding docs. Used as a clean-room starting point when recovering from a broken dev environment or onboarding a new agent instance. The bill-memories.json file here is a Vestige memory export snapshot.
key_files: .env.local — Minimal environment config for recovery bootstrap
service-account.json — Recovery Firebase credentials (separate from main)
AARON-CHECKLIST.txt — Manual recovery steps checklist
bill-memories.json — Vestige memory export (Bill's knowledge snapshot)

email-logs-backup.description: Local backup of email log data exported from Firestore. Contains JSON snapshots of email_logs collection documents taken during debugging of the email delivery system. Read-only reference data.

migration-logs.description: Logs and output files from Firestore data migration operations. Records what was migrated, any errors encountered, and validation results. Useful for post-migration verification and rollback planning.

perf-data.description: Performance testing data files. Contains HAR captures from browser network sessions used by scripts/analyze-firestore.js and scripts/analyze-har.js to map Firestore query patterns and identify performance issues (unbounded reads, N+1 queries).
